rdf:Description.dc:publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / beta maṣāḥǝft Brill Brill, IslHornAfr: Islam in the Horn of Africa, A Comparative Literary Approach. European Research Council (Advanced Grant no. 322849)
rdf:Description.dc:contributor: P. Bearman, Th. Bianquis, C.E. Bosworth, E. van Donzel, W.P. Heinrichs
rdf:Description.foaf:name: الدمياطي, نور الدين
al-Dimyāṭī, Nūr al-Dīn
الدمياطي, اصيل الدين
al-Dimyāṭī, Aṣīl al-Dīn

crm:E52_Time-span.crm:P78_is_identified_by: not before the end of the 7th/13th centuryHis dates are uncertain but almost certainly not before the end of the 7th/13th century; author of a qaṣīda in lām on the names of God; each verse of this qaṣīda is reputed to possess mysterious virtues, given in detail by the commentaries of which the text has several times been the object. The qaṣīda Dimyāṭiyya holds a considerable place in the worship of the semiliterate, in particular in North Africa. A fragment of another work of the same kind attributed to al-Dimyāṭī is preserved, with a commentary, in the ms. Paris, B.N. Arabe 1050, fol. 138-139, while an imitation, not without vulgarisms in its language, was written by a certain Maḥmūd Hizza al-Dimyāṭī (printed as an appendix to Badīʿ al-maḳāl by an anonymous Andalusian, with the title of al-Istiġfār al-asmā fī naẓm asmāʾ Allāh al-ḥusnā, Būlāḳ 1319/1901).
